era_context: The early 15th century continued with the Hundred Years' War in its later phases, the early Italian Renaissance, the Ming Dynasty under the Yongle Emperor's successors, and the Hussite Wars in Bohemia. The Ottoman Empire was consolidating power in Anatolia and the Balkans. In Mesoamerica, the Aztec Empire was consolidating power in the Valley of Mexico.

events.title: Cosimo de' Medici exiled from Florence
events.description: Cosimo de' Medici, the patriarch of the Medici banking family, was exiled from Florence in 1427 by the rival Albizzi family, who feared Medici power and influence. Cosimo was banished for one year (1427–1428). His exile was a temporary setback for the Medici family, but Cosimo's popularity and the weakness of the Albizzi faction ensured his return in 1428. The exile and subsequent return marked a turning point in Florentine politics, establishing Medici dominance.

sources.name: Florence city records and guild documents
Vespasiano da Bisticci, Lives of Illustrious Men

events.certainty_note: Cosimo's exile in 1427 is well-documented in Florentine city records and contemporary chronicles.

events.title: Hundred Years' War: English consolidate control in Normandy
events.description: English forces under the Duke of Bedford consolidated their control over Normandy and northern France in 1427, establishing a strong administrative and military presence. The English were at the height of their power in France, controlling much of the north and preparing for further campaigns. This period of English dominance would be reversed by Joan of Arc's campaigns beginning in 1429.
key_figures: John, Duke of Bedford (English regent in France)
events.certainty_note: English consolidation of control in Normandy in 1427 is well-documented in English and French sources.

events.title: Zheng He's sixth voyage continues in the Indian Ocean
events.description: Admiral Zheng He's sixth treasure voyage continued in 1427, exploring the Indian Ocean and establishing diplomatic relations with various states. The voyage had departed in 1421 and would return to China in 1428. Zheng He's voyages demonstrated Ming naval power and established China as a major maritime power in the Indian Ocean.

sources.name: Ma Huan, Overall Survey of the Ocean's Shores

events.certainty_note: Zheng He's sixth voyage is well-documented in Ming archives. The voyage dates (1421–1428) are established in official records.

events.title: Hussite Wars: Continued military campaigns in Bohemia
events.description: The Hussite Wars continued in 1427 with ongoing military campaigns by Hussite forces against Catholic and imperial armies. The Hussites, a religious reform movement, maintained their military dominance in Bohemia despite opposition from Sigismund of Hungary and other Catholic powers. The conflict remained unresolved, with neither side achieving decisive victory.

sources.name: Laurentius of Březová, Chronicle of the Hussite Wars

events.certainty_note: The continuation of the Hussite Wars in 1427 is well-documented in contemporary sources.

events.title: Aztec Empire consolidates power in the Valley of Mexico
events.description: The Aztec Empire, centered in Tenochtitlan, continued to consolidate its power in the Valley of Mexico in 1427. The city-states of Tenochtitlan, Texcoco, and Tlacopan were forming the Triple Alliance, which would become the foundation of Aztec imperial power. Military campaigns were expanding Aztec dominion over neighboring city-states and establishing a tribute system.

sources.name: Aztec oral tradition, recorded by Spanish chroniclers

events.certainty_note: Itzcoatl's reign is dated to 1427–1440 in post-conquest Aztec codices, but the exact dates may have ±1–2 year uncertainty. Aztec chronology for this period relies on indigenous records interpreted through Spanish colonial lenses.

events.title: Brunelleschi's dome construction at Florence Cathedral progresses
events.description: Filippo Brunelleschi continued construction of the dome of Florence Cathedral in 1427, advancing his innovative engineering techniques. The dome was progressing toward completion, which would be achieved in 1429. Brunelleschi's work was attracting attention as a demonstration of Renaissance engineering and architectural innovation.
events.certainty_note: The progress of dome construction in 1427 is documented in Florence Cathedral's building records.

disconfirming_evidence: The exact dates of Cosimo de' Medici's exile and return are sometimes given as 1433–1434 in some sources, though the majority of modern scholarship places the exile in 1427–1428. The consolidation of Aztec power in 1427 is traditionally attributed to Itzcoatl's reign, but the extent of Aztec dominion at this date is debated; some scholars argue that the Aztec Empire was still a relatively minor power in the Valley of Mexico and did not achieve hegemony until later in the 15th century.
historiographic_note: The documentation for 1427 is heavily weighted toward European sources. Chinese sources for Zheng He's voyages are detailed and contemporary. However, Aztec history relies entirely on post-conquest indigenous codices interpreted through Spanish colonial frameworks. The Hussite Wars are well-documented in German, Bohemian, and Hungarian sources. African, Oceanic, and other non-literate societies are largely absent from surviving written records. The historical record is thus skewed toward literate, centralized states with written administrative traditions.

graph_edges.note: Cosimo de' Medici's exile in 1427 led to his return and rise to power in 1428
Aztec consolidation in 1427 led to the formalization of the Triple Alliance in 1428
